accounting_period.detail: The first public ledger closes one public accounting window under the current decentralized network cadence and records both positive work and penalties in the same retained period.

contribution_entries.detail: Apple MLX earns the miner share because the accepted validator receipt proves a positive contribution for the live public-miner window.
Google earns the larger validator share because it supplied the accepted challenge and score receipt for the canonical miner contribution.
Apple MLX still earns a smaller validator accounting share because replay-required work remains billable validator labor even when it blocks promotion.
Google earns one checkpoint-authority share for retaining and serving the held checkpoint candidate under the current multi-validator decision.
RunPod earns the secondary checkpoint-authority share because the decentralized network still retains it as a mirrored durable authority.

penalty_entries.detail: The first ledger deducts the slashed RTX 4080 miner amount directly from the provisional reward pool so the retained ledger says both who earned and who was penalized.

final_allocations.detail: Apple MLX combines miner and validator earnings into the largest net allocation for the window.
Google combines validator and checkpoint-authority earnings into the second-largest net allocation for the window.
RunPod retains one smaller checkpoint-authority allocation for mirrored state retention.
The slashed RTX 4080 miner carries a negative net allocation and no payout weight because the duplicate-work incident crossed the current fraud threshold.

claim_boundary: This contract freezes the first tamper-evident contribution ledger: one accounting period, retained gross work entries, retained penalty entries, and payout-ready net allocations. It does not yet publish those outcomes outside the local accounting surface.
